title: Lily of the Nile Shawlby Michele DuNaier
description: Inspired by the flower of the same name, the Lily of the Nile shawl is an elegant yet easy-to-crochet project. The tall and stately flower, with its dainty curved petals, is evoked by the shawl's dramatic lines and delicate edging points. The shawl starts at the back of the neck and is worked downward to the lacy flower petal edging. Increasing is done at the row ends and at three strategic points in the body rows to create a triple spine structure. DC rows are interspersed with rows of Filet crochet for visual interest.
Needle / Hook Sizes: US 7 (4.50mm) Crochet Hook & US G/6 (4.00mm) for Lower and Top Edgings

title: Linked Loops Super Scarfby Lisa Wicker
description: With lofty texture and dramatic length, this super scarf makes a bold statement while delivering generous warmth. The pattern is worked in the Linked Double Crochet (LDC) stitch while holding two strands of bulky yarn together. This variation of the Double Crochet (DC) stitch links double crochets together to create a denser fabric with fewer holes. The result is a cozy statement piece—whether wrapped, draped, or belted. While the downy softness of Wonderfluff is preferred, any two strands of bulky yarn held together can be used. Yarn usage and finished measurements may vary.

title: Linwood Place Blanketby Rachel Beth
description: A twist on the traditional modular crochet blanket, the Linwood Place Blanket uses filet diamonds and special cluster stitches to add texture and interest. Each square is worked in the round and features a filet pattern surrounded by special cluster stitches. As a modular design, any rectangular arrangement of squares can be joined together before working the border. The border features a special cluster stitch round and ends with a round of shells created using the same cluster stitch. The border can be worked around any rectangular arrangement of squares. The border adds 4'' to the length and width.

title: Lisbeth Beanieby Sarah Elrashidi
description: Inspired by the icy landscape of a Colorado winter, the arching pattern in this hat mirrors the wind whipping snow into drifts. The Lisbeth Beanie is worked in the round from the bottom up with a ribbed brim. The top of the hat is stitched with a repeating arch and then cinched together before being topped with a faux fur pom-pom.

title: Little Bear Coatby Brenda K.B. Anderson
description: Could a baby possibly be cuddlier? This Little Bear Coat features an ample hood complete with adorable bear ears. The double-breasted front opening and simple ribbed stitch pattern make this unisex sweater an instant classic. Extra long sleeves can be cuffed until baby needs the extra length. This coat is worked in turned rows, with very minimal shaping in simple single crochet ribbing, making this project suitable for advanced beginners. Work the hood and sweater fronts as one from the top down: one piece for the right and one piece for the left. Small crocheted discs (stitched to the wrong side of Right Front) reinforce button attachment points.

title: Log Cabin Dishclothby Tian Connaughton
description: Play with colors, use up scraps with this fun and quick log cabin inspired dishcloth. Each new color is built off of the squares created before, turning the piece clockwise and working into the ends of each row. Weaving in ends as you go will save time at the end!

title: Lotus Headbandby Tasha Margette Reynolds
description: The luxurious feel of a spa day is at your fingertips. The soothing sounds of flowing water, soft and cozy towels, warm steam on your face. The soothing headband is comprised of three delicate strips stitched together creating a lacy, textured feel.
Sizes Included: 21" circ x 3.25" wide, after blocking & assembly

title: Love Squared C2C Pillowby Quayln Stark
description: You’ll just love this adorable pixelated heart pillow, using a simple Corner-to-Corner (C2C) crochet chart that you can customize with your own colorways or background design. If you’re new to C2C, this is a great place to start—we know that you’ll love it! Love Squared C2C Pillow is a simple charted corner to corner pattern with the option of customization. Two separate square panels are made then seamed together over a 12’’ form to make a chic throw pillow. Chart one is a pre-made rainbow heart chart, whereas chart two has a blank and customizable background.

title: Lovely Lichen Pillowby Alyssa George
description: Simple stripes and gorgeous textures! This bold, modern throw pillow is sure to be a statement piece of decor in any room of your home. The Lovely Lichen Pillow is crocheted in the Crystal Wave pattern, a repetitive pattern of single, front-post single, and double crochet stitches, a combination that makes an amazing texture. The front and back are worked separately with the back panel being worked as two pieces. These overlapping pieces on the back create a pocket so the pillow insert can easily be removed.

title: Lovestruck Jar Cozyby Ilaria Caliri
description: Use crochet thread to make a modern boho-style jar cozy, which makes the ideal wedding table decor. Fill it with flowers, plants, or a candle to light up a special night. The Lovestruck Jar Cozy is worked in the round from the bottom up. The vertical pattern is made of X-shaped motifs, clusters, and front post stitches to get a textured effect that emphasizes verticality. The top edge is enriched with a fringe and an optional drawstring band.
Sizes Included: To cover 3.5" diameter jar, adaptable to any size

title: Luxe Flowers Cowlby Maria O'Keefe
description: Lace and hand painted silk is such a luxury but should be an everyday indulgence with this slinky easy to wear with everything cowl. Cowl is made up of 56 hexagonal motifs that are joined-as-you-go (jaygo). Before crocheting last motifs together, pin ends together and try on to determine best length for you. To make a longer cowl, just add more motifs.

title: Mae Wrap Dressby Brynnan Foster
description: Happiness is made with soft, cushy yarn; simplicity is enjoyed through classic, beautiful design. Find both with your little one and delight in this darling wrap dress for any occasion. Body is crocheted in 3 pieces with the front panels worked continuously from top down, in rows. Ties are added to front panels and worked in rows. The back is worked top down in rows starting with the lower portion; the upper portion and one back shoulder is worked onto the lower portion; the remaining back shoulder is worked onto the upper portion. All panels are sewn together at the shoulders and sides, leaving an opening on the side to thread one tie through.

Shrug is made in four separate pieces, each worked back–and–forth in rows from bottom up. Once blocked, the pieces are sewn together to create the garment. title: Mandala Buntingby Helen Anderson
description: These crochet mandalas can be worked up as individual wall hangings or multiple mandalas can be combined to create a beautiful statement bunting, making a gorgeous hanging wedding decoration or a lovely handmade gift. title: Marimo Pillowsby Brynnan Foster

title: Marvelous Marl Hooded Scarfby 10 Hours or Less
description: Combine two different yarns to make your own marl in this quick and colorful hooded scarf! Worked in basic single crochet without any shaping, the simple rectangular shape is ideal for beginners. Keep it as-is for a classic scarf or stitch a single seam to form a head-hugging hood. Scarf is worked vertically in rows in one piece from long edge to long edge. Upon completion, scarf is folded in half and seamed at top to create the hood. Work entire scarf with one strand each of bulky weight yarn and fingering weight yarn held together as one.

title: Medley Shawlby Handmade with Chrissy
description: Add a little shabby to your chic with the Medley Shawl. title: Midtown Mantle Mock-neck Topby Liz Salazar
description: Elevate while you accessorize with this side-button top that features a traditional braided cable pattern alongside a feathered fan pattern that mixes things up! Midtown Mantle is worked bottom up in 2 separate panels, then joined at shoulders. Bottom/neck ribbing and button band are worked after panels have been seamed. Ch-1 and ch-2 at beg of row/rnd does not count as a st. Blocking, especially the fans, is crucial for this project.

title: Mighty Mittensby Helen Anderson
description: These quick and easy colorful mittens make a great gift or a perfect treat for yourself. Have fun playing with these vibrant color changes and work them up in any color palette of your choice. Crocheted in Half Double Crochet, the mittens are worked from the bottom up and in joined rounds. Yarn is re-joined to work the thumb.

title: Miley Bagby Smitha Crasta
description: This simple yet chic bag looks stunning and is the perfect size to toss in your essentials and take it along to the beach. The cute tie details and the two tones are sure to grab attention and give a beautiful look to a summery outfit. Dishie (worsted, 100% Cotton; 190yds/100g): MC Inlet 29504; 1 ball C1 Silver 25789; 1 ball

title: Mini Amigurumi Astronautby Lion Brand
description: The Amigurumi Astronaut is a crochet pattern made with medium weight yarn that has a skill level of easy. Some yarns used in the sample are not available on Crochet.com. title: Mini Moon Potted Cactusby Handmade with Chrissy
description: Adorn your home, desk, or windowsill and never worry about watering again! Make an adorable potted cactus for yourself and all your friends. The perfect gift for green thumbs and not-so-green thumbs alike. Unlike standard amigurumi, this cactus is worked in double crochet to give it an extra plush and squishy feel. The pot and main cactus are worked as one piece in joined rounds and include front post stitches to provide extra shaping and texture. The flower is then stitched as a separate piece and finished with a simple French knot for an added pop of color.

title: Mismatched Dishcloth (Free Download)by Stacey Winklepleck
description: This dishcloth pattern includes both waves & chevrons with mismatched stripes for a unique look for your dishcloth. In this pattern, you will simply switch colors every 2 rows; using four colors will give you a wave in each color but this is an easy pattern to customize to as many or as few colors as you would like. One of the colors used in the sample is no longer available; we have made a similar color suggestion in the Kit Builder.

title: Misty Morning Mosaic Cowlby Brooke Byars
description: This cowl was inspired by the texture of a honeycomb. The Honeycomb Stitch and ombre color changes in the yarn give the cowl a geometric striped effect with endless color opportunities. The pattern is worked in the round utilizing the Tunisian Honeycomb stitch to create a geometric textured effect. This piece is worked in a continuous spiral with a double-ended hook to create a seamless loop that is easily adjustable to fit your style.
Needle / Hook Sizes: Two Size 7mm Interchangeable Tunisian crochet hooks with a 24” cord, or a double ended 7mm hook

title: Misty Rose Dishclothby Kim Cameron
description: Misty Rose is a fanciful version of a crocheted dishcloth to decorate your kitchen with as well as having a purpose. Pick two colors and create a blossom of your own. This pattern is fairly quick to crochet and perfect to give as a gift. Color used in the sample is no longer available; we have made a similar color suggestion in the yarn calculator below.

title: Modern Mug Rugsby Sheila Houle
description: This pattern includes detailed instructions and pictures to make a set of four mug rugs with a clean and modern design that compliment any style of decor. Each mug rug is worked using tapestry crochet which creates a durable piece. The only thing left to add is your favorite drink in your prettiest mug.

title: Montague Avenue Hat & Cowlby Miroslava Mihalkova
description: Winter weather calls for rich textures and warmth in crochet items and this hat and cowl set, combining bobbles and cables, ticks all the boxes. The Montague Avenue hat and cowl are worked flat along the long edge with both items being seamed at the end. The hat and cowl feature Front Post Double Crochet ribbing, diamond bobble pattern, and cables using Front Post Double and Treble crochet. Optional: finish the hat with a faux fur pom-pom!

title: Morning Mocha Ruanaby Leshia Tweddle
description: Staying warm on a cool day just got a little more stylish with the Morning Mocha Ruana. Worked up in a bulky weight wool yarn you will get all of the great properties of wool in a beautiful one size fits most layering piece. Completed in three panels using the Linen Stitch, this is sure to be a go-to for gifting. Wool of the Andes™ Superwash (bulky, 100% Superwash Wool; 137yds/100g): MC Oyster Heather 26516; 7 hanks C1 Briar Heather 26497; 5 hanks C2 Camel Heather 26498; 1 hank

title: Morningside Hatby Lion Brand
description: The subtle striping of neutral and pink in cozy, Feels Like a Dream yarn make this knit hat the perfect cold weather accessory. This bulky yarn works up fast and can be machine washed and dried for easy care! To support our Independent Designers , this pattern is excluded from promotions.

title: Moroccan Tile Pouffe & Cushionby Tiam Safari
description: Inject some color and boho chic into your home with this duo of reversible Moroccan tile-inspired pieces, worked in bulky yarn and bright shades to add a quick spot of color to your decor. The Moroccan Tile Pouffe & Cushion is worked in a simple mandala with bobbles, scallops, surface crochet, and alternating basic crochet stitches used to create bold lines and contrast.

title: Mosaic Crochet Tote Bagby Sara Dudek
description: Use brightly colored yarn to create colorful geometric patterns using the mosaic crochet technique. You'll love the visual impact of this fun mosaic crochet tote bag and will want to take it everywhere. If you are new to mosaic crochet, this is the perfect chance to practice reading a mosaic crochet chart and implementing the mosaic technique into a fun and playful tote bag. Use basic single crochet stitches for the body of the bag and then work the mosaic technique to create the patterning at the top. Mix it up with fun colors if you're feeling playful.

title: Mountain Meadow Cowl and Mittsby Jen Dwyer
description: Designed for gentle walks in the woods with a steaming cup of coffee, the Mountain Meadows Cowl and Fingerless Mitts set features a traditional crochet stitch pattern in a modern color scheme. Waves of cream scallops alternate on a background of gradually darkening shades of brown. The cowl is worked in the round with yarn carried up each round, minimizing ends to weave in. The fingerless mitts are worked in rows beginning with ribbing. A simple increase on each end creates the thumbhole before finishing the finger area.

title: Mountain Top Cardiganby Two of Wands
description: Head to such great heights with the Mountaintop Cardigan. Chunky crochet gives this sweater coat a structured, protective silhouette that's jazzed up with fun colorwork and pockets. To support our Independent Designers , this pattern is excluded from promotions.
Sizes Included: XS - 22" back width, Sm - 26" back width, M - 26" back width, L - 28.75" back width, XL - 30.25" back width, 2XL - 32" back width, 3XL - 34.5" back width, 4XL - 36.25" back width

title: Multi Chevron Dishclothby Sara Dudek
description: Use basic tapestry crochet to create a fashion-forward look when you use Dishie Multi yarn. Its variegated color palettes bring a surprise element when used to create a stylish chevron pattern.

title: Multiverse Colorwork Hatby WeCrochet Design Team
description: Did you know that you can use your crochet skills to create a traditional multicolor hat? Simply choose your colorwork pattern, gather your yarn, and off you go! This hat begins with the ribbed brim worked sideways. The ribbed section is seamed to create a ring, and then stitches are picked up along one side and worked upwards to the crown.

title: Nacelle Project Basketby Tiam Safari
description: Inspired by boho-style woven handbags and baskets from the island of Mallorca, this crochet pom-pom basket is perfect for storing your projects in a chic way and will be a fun and timeless addition to your home decor. Holding DK weight yarn tripled, this basket is worked in the round over three strands of super bulky yarn to provide body and structure. The handles are worked into the top of the basket, and the optional pompoms are sewn on at the end.

title: Nanala Hatby Leshia Tweddle
description: Add a gorgeous and functional accessory to every outfit this spring and summer. The Nanala Hat is worked in rounds from the top down. The front post stitch swirl pattern and hat band bobble stitches create interesting textural details, while the wide brim will keep you shaded and comfortable. YARN Cotlin (DK, 70% Tanguis Cotton, 30% Linen; 123yards/100g) Conch 25776, 4 skeins
Sizes Included: Inner band circumference, 21.25 x 6.75 length, brim depth 3.25, Inner band circumference, 22.5 x 7.25" length, brim depth 3.25

title: Newlywed Bannerby Dawn Curran
description: Inspired by the chic and creative boho wedding trend, this Newlywed Banner is one element you can add to your d é cor that will help make the day romantic and memorable. The pattern incorporates intarsia crochet to create the letters to spell MR & MRS (or mix and match to fit your preferred titles). A lace panel is worked along the lower edge of the flag and is finished with a picot border. Each flag is worked separately, making it an accessible project for crochet makers.

title: Newport Toteby Kathleen Berlew

title: Nightfall Sweaterby Natasha Robarge
description: Inspired by the curve of a classical vase with ornate decorations, the Nightfall Sweater features an array of light cables against a chain mesh background. Ribbing is worked with a smaller hook for slight shaping. The front and back are split in half in the middle for the neck and worked straight up. When the halves part, they create a v-neck opening, shoulder slopes, and a slight hi-lo effect at the hem. Shoulders are seamed. Drop-shoulder sleeves are worked separately and seamed to the body. The height of the post stitches is varied to achieve an airy elongated look and balance gauge across different sections. The Body section is worked in turned rows. The Border is worked in rnds around the body of the shawl.

It incorporates basic stitches as well as a few more unusual stitches for ultimate luxury that is fun to stitch.

The ornament cover piece is worked around a plastic crafting orb. The plaid ribbon is worked with three alternating colors, tied in a bow, and attached to finish off the ornament.

The main body is worked first in the main color followed by the trim and faux-insert collar in a contrasting color. Next, the hood is worked flat onto the main body and seamed at the top to close. Lastly, the sleeves are added to the main body, working in turned rounds. Pockets and buttons are added to complete the look.

title: Please Stand By Bagby Brenda K.B. Anderson
description: An homage to televisions of the past, this messenger-style bag is the perfect size to transport your iPad or actual notepad. The front and back are worked in turned rounds beginning at the center. The side (thickness) is worked in joined, turned rounds leaving a section open for the zipper. The Front, Back, and Side are all backed in plastic canvas for structure.

description: Layers are essential through the fall and winter seasons. Layers that are easy to add and remove are key to being prepared. title: Popcorn Throwby Kristen Clay
description: Basic stitches are combined in a special way to create a simple but sweet throw with a pop of texture in the wide border, suitable for adults and kiddos alike! The striking border is worked as you go, which means the first and last group of rows are entirely the border stitch, while the body of the throw has each row starting and ending with the border stitch. The flowers and leaves are crocheted and then sewn on. Follow the pictured color and placement scheme or make it your own for a unique fashion statement.

Double Crochet rounds are alternated with wrong side Single Crochet and plump Puff Stitch rounds - the stitch variety and four coordinated colors make for an interesting crochet project.

title: Snowflake Granny Square Bagby Iassia Kryvonos
description: Who doesn’t love granny squares? Featuring an iconic winter motif over a solid stitch, these squares are ideal for stylish bags during the winter season. Made from sturdy worsted wool, they work up fast, creating the perfect texture for a durable tote! The squares can be joined in two ways to create a classical “tulip” bag or a large pouch with a button closure. Join contrasting squares for a checkerboard pattern or stick to the same background color on all squares for a uniform finish. Regardless of the choice, both options look stunning.

title: Snowman Door Hangerby Nicole Chase
description: Brighten up your door with this Snowman Door Hanger. With deconstructed pieces, this unique decoration is a fun gift idea or addition to your own home this winter. title: Snuggle Stitch Stripes Dishclothby Heather Mann
description: Create a textured dishcloth with lots of room for color play. This special stitch makes for a super-absorbent cloth that will become your go-to for dishcloths that make great housewarming gifts.

title: Soft Serve Cabled Beanieby Brenda K.B. Anderson
description: Squishy, twisting cables, swirl around this tweed beanie, reminiscent of soft-serve ice cream with sprinkles! Easy enough to be a great introduction to cables, this cute hat has style to spare. The generous ribbing folds up to create an extra layer of warmth, and the cables rise straight up to the top of the hat, thanks to the smooth, perfectly vertical extended single crochet stitches between post stitches.

title: Solomon's Knot Teeby Sati Glenn

title: Sparkles the Unicorn Softieby Brenda K.B. Anderson
description: Have you hugged your unicorn today? This extraordinary creature is said to have magical healing powers within its horn. And who doesn't need a bit of magic from time to time? The rainbow hooves and mane are extra cheerful, perking up any room where this unicorn resides. The spiraling horn happens as a result of a few increases right next to a few decreases. Be careful not to miss the first half of the first decrease as it's easy to miss. Within the directions for the hooves are instruction for a join that reduces the appearance of a jog in color. A little crocheted strip, made in rainbow colors takes the guesswork out of adding the mane to the head. Simply attach the fringe to the mane following the colors in the strip, then sew the strip to the head.

title: Spring Fresh Lapghanby Lion Brand

title: Stay Cozy Beanieby Iassia Kryvonos
description: Dive into the world of crochet texture with this snuggly and irresistibly warm Stay Cozy Beanie. This crochet hat is stitched in the round, from the bottom up. Wonderfluff puffs emerge from narrow ribbing and gather elegantly at the crown. Play with two tonal or contrasting colors of yarn, fashioning long “spikelets” and amazing texture in this crochet hat. title: Stitch Your State: Warm Up America!by Knit Picks Design Team
description: The Great Yarn Challenge (TGYC) is hosted by the Craft Yarn Council and is a series of challenges based around different themes. Knit Picks and WeCrochet partnered with CYC to bring you the Stitch Your State: Warm Up America! challenge. We have designed 51 charts (1 for each state plus DC) to be used in a variety of ways. Each measures 9"x7" so they are perfect for Warm Up America! blocks. You can find more information about The Great Yarn Challenge HERE. There are basic instructions for both knit and crochet to get you started and to finish, then you pick your state chart to work from. For these charts, the white boxes are the background, and the dark boxes are the shape of the state. We've also included a yellow box for each state capital! Once you have finished your state, now the fun begins! Choose some images to show what your state means to you - you can use simple images such as clip art or go as detailed as you want! Use state flowers, state birds, tourist attractions and anything that makes your state home. Having trouble coming up with some images? Here's what we did: For our home state of Washington, we wanted to represent our office in Vancouver and naturally, the Space Needle in Seattle. And, of course, we had to include some trees for the Evergreen state! Ohio is as familiar to us as Washington is, since our warehouse is there! It's represented with Cedar Point Amusement Park. We've also included the Rock n' Roll Hall of fame in Cleveland (rocks!) and the state flower, the Red Carnation. Designer Alison Griffith used to spend vacations in South Carolina watching the dolphins, so she had to include that to represent SC. The state flag features a palmetto tree and a white crescent so those are stitched here too!

title: Striped Belt Bagby Lion Brand

Easily adjust the size or length by working more stitches or rows to customize your sweater to your perfect fit.

Sizes Included: 37" square - Cheery, 37" square - Rainbow, 37" square - Sunset

title: Tuff Puff Rainbow Wall Hangingby Heather Mann
description: This boho-inspired wall hanging is quick to crochet and makes a great home dec accent for a variety of rooms. Each row of the pattern is worked in a new color, with every row as a RS row. Begin each row on the same side and fasten off at end of every row.
Sizes Included: 12'' x 14'' w/o fringe (12'' x 28'' w/ fringe)

title: Tunisian Full Stitch Cowlby Anne Van Winkle
description: A beautiful cowl cascading in length makes for the perfect fall and winter accessory. This accessory is a wonderful mix of regular crochet for the ribbing and Tunisian crochet for the body of the cowl. Made with hand dyed DK weight yarn, this cowl is a must make for an easy to wear accessory.
Sizes Included: 12” long x 20” (top / neck circ) and 26” (bottom circ.) after blocking
Needle / Hook Sizes: US J/10 (6.0mm) double-ended Tunisian Crochet hook with varying cord lengths up to 18”; US I/9 (5.5mm) crochet hook for the ribbing

title: Tunisian Lattice Dishclothby Sarah Nairalez
description: The Tunisian Lattice dishcloth offers simple grace to your dishcloth collection. The thick fabric created by the Tunisian stitches helps make a secure and absorbent dishcloth.

title: Tunisian Monstera Blanket and Pillowby Miroslava Mihalkova
description: This monstera leaf blanket and pillow bring rich and vibrant colors to your home directly from the tropical forests of southern Mexico. Make yourself a refreshing drink and let them take you on an adventure, minus the mosquitos and all the crawling and creeping creatures! The blanket and both sides of the pillow are worked flat with RS always facing. Both blanket and cushion start with a border in C2, while the monstera leaf is worked in MC and C1.
Sizes Included: Blanket 63" wide x 57.5" high; Pillow 18" square
Needle / Hook Sizes: 7mm Tunisian crochet hook with 16" cable for pillow, 60" cable for blanket

title: Twilight Topby Kabila Sri Ponnusamy
description: Inspired by the openwork of a garden trellis, this design creates the same effect with lacy stitches, giving the fabric great drape. The yoke of the garment delights the eye with a textural contrasting stitch pattern. The Twilight Top is a quick and easy project with a circular yoke and top-down seamless construction. It is worked in continuous rounds using a double ended-hook.
Sizes Included: 36.25" finished chest circumference, 40.5" finished chest circumference, 44.25" finished chest circumference, 48.5" finished chest circumference, 52.5" finished chest circumference
Needle / Hook Sizes: U.S. J/10 (6 mm) double-ended crochet hook

title: Twin Rainbow Throwby Chrisandra Skipper
description: This beautiful throw is a mix of minimalism and boho. The rainbow pops, both in color and dimension, off the blanket's simple texture as the faux fur adds just a touch of extra softness. The Twin Rainbow Throw is a rectangular blanket, worked flat from the short end with one stitch and no colorwork. The rainbow is added after the blanket is worked up using surface stitches. Full tassels are the finishing touch! Some colors used in the sample are no longer available; we have made color suggestions in the kit builder below.

title: Two-Color Cable Cowlby Amy Gunderson
description: An all-over pattern of post stitches creates a geometric effect in this snuggly cowl. The pattern is written for two sizes, the larger of which can be wrapped twice around your neck. This cowl is worked in the round. Each round of pattern is worked twice - once in MC and a second time in CC. Unless otherwise noted, throughout the cabled section, MC stitches are only worked into MC stitches while CC stitches are only worked into CC stitches.

title: Two Color Hatby Lion Brand
description: The Two-Color Hat is a crochet hat pattern made with super bulky yarn that has a skill level of easy.

title: Ultimate Crochet Palette Blanketby Allison Griffith
description: Crochet this full-sized afghan, using each color of our beloved Palette! Each square measures 5"; using all 150 colors creates a beautiful rainbow throw measuring 50" x 75". Each square is worked from the inside out with each color, bordered by a round of white. Use the suggested color layout or create your own personal colorway - the possibilities are endless! As shown, this pattern uses every color of our Palette line. Each square uses approximately 10 grams of yarn or about 50 yards of the main color. You will need a total of 6 skeins of White for the border, as well as sewing the squares together. This pattern also uses a Size D (3.25mm) crochet hook. See all of our Palette colors here as well as our complete 150 Palette Sampler.

title: Unidentified Mooing Objectsby Megan Kreiner
description: Test your dexterity with this fun and clever game. Whimsical and sweet, you won’t mind helping our alien friend catch the fluffy round cow. Animation™ (DK, 100% Mercerized Cotton; 139yds/50g), 1 skein each C1: Butterscotch N2679 C2: Swan N2700 C3: Linen N2693 C4: White N2702 C5: Coffee N2682 C6: Jade N2689 C7: Inlet N2688 C8: Black N2675 C9: Azure N2674 Alternatively, Animation™ Amigurumi Set (DK, 100% Mercerized Cotton; 698yds/250g) in Flora & Fauna  29876 and  Primary Colors 29878 may be substituted.
Sizes Included: Alien and Cow (complete set), Alien 4" x 7" circ (head and UFO), Cow: 3" tall x 3" long (nose to tail) x 2.5" wide (side to side)

title: Urban Garden Throwby Maria O'Keefe
description: Crocheting with different color combinations is always fun but when 24 curated colors are available in a mini-pack, playing with color becomes effortless. With so many color combinations possible, it's hard to put the project down once you start. Hexagon motifs are joined-as-you-go (jaygo) on all six sides. Half-hexagon motifs are added to the sides after all full-size hexagon motifs are joined. A simple edging completes the throw which is then finished with fringe.

title: Vanilla Latte Super Scarfby Kathleen Berlew
description: This generously sized scarf makes a versatile addition to any winter wardrobe and a thoughtful handmade gift. It can be worn long, wrapped multiple times around the neck, or even pulled over the head as a hood. Worked lengthwise, the scarf features alternating rows of paired single crochet stitches, which create subtle ridges, and treble crochet stitches that give the design a frothy feel. The resulting fabric's tendency to lean to one side can be corrected by blocking.

title: Varsity Scarfby Kathleen Berlew
description: Sports fans on your holiday list will love a cozy, stylish scarf customized in their favorite team’s colors. The easy, endlessly adaptable design is perfect for those who are less enthusiastic about sports, too--just switch the color scheme to match their favorite fashion palette.

title: Verdura Bagby Kate Alinari
description: Pale honeydew meets vivid green in this showpiece bag. Simple and stylish, it goes with everyday summer outfits. Essentially made with 2 disks and 1 strip seamed together (plus handles), it's easy and fun to make. Leaves are worked separately and then sewn onto the bag. Felt lining makes the bag sturdy and non-stretching. No specific sewing skills are needed.

title: Victoria Falls Throwby Tiam Safari
description: Inspired by heavy cotton tasseled throws from multiple cultures, this chevron blanket worked in tones of neutral and grey is a DIY take on a timeless classic. Worked in a chevron pattern of single crochet, and designed for basic crochet skills, this throw is sure to be a firm fave. Easy to memorize, the pattern is perfect for working up while watching a movie!

title: Vista Wrapby Two of Wands
description: Crochet your favorite vista into this meditative wrap designed to represent the view that lights you up. Whether it’s the calming horizon of your home or a faraway vision in your dreams, choose a color palette that speaks to your soul and indulge in the simple stitches and textures that transport you there.

title: Waffle Stitch Throwby Lion Brand
description: The Waffle Stitch Afghan is a knit afghan pattern made with super bulky weight yarn that has a skill level of easy. To support our Independent Designers , this pattern is excluded from promotions.

title: Washington Park Cowl-Neck Sweaterby Natasha Robarge
description: There's always something to do at the park! Whether you're watching kids at the playground or walking the trails, you need something cute to wear on your outing. The Washington Park Cowl Neck Sweater is fun to make and even more fun to wear: a variety of stitches will keep you entertained throughout the project. Front, back, and sleeves are worked separately from bottom up. Sleeves are easily seamed to body in a drop shoulder construction. Relaxed fit supports a variety of sizes and preferred ease. The sweater grows fast with medium weight yarn and a 5mm hook, and the fabric is light and pliable. Wide cowl neck seamlessly continues from body to collar.
Sizes Included: 40.5" finished chest, 43.5" finished chest, 47" finished chest, 50" finished chest, 53.5" finished chest, 56.5" finished chest

title: Waves Afghanby Lion Brand
description: This easy afghan is fun and functional - a perfect project for a winter day. Afghan is worked in rows, changing yarn color following a Stripe Sequence.

title: Wedded Wall Hangingby Kristen Clay
description: This filet crochet wall d é cor features French-style scroll motifs making it the perfect versatile wall hanging when worked up in delicate thread. This project is perfect for hanging as decoration at a wedding, outdoor garden party, or in your home. The wall hanging is worked from the top down, using just a few basic stitches and a simple chart. Follow the chart to create the patterning, and attach to a dowel or branch for hanging.
Sizes Included: 5.5" x 14.5, excluding tassel and hanging ribbon

title: Wedding Planner Dressby Natasha Robarge
description: Exquisite fabric in diamond lace with ornate picots makes this comfortable summer dress worthy of special occasions. The inspiration for this dress was the work of a wedding planner at a beach wedding who tends to her duties in a beautiful and stylish but functional and easy-to-wear garment. The dress will also look edgy and chic with leggings. The dress is worked top down. The construction allows length adjustments for better fit and individual style. Oversized sleeves are worked as part of the front and back in one piece.
Sizes Included: 40" finished chest measurement, 45" finished chest measurement, 48" finished chest measurement, 52" finished chest measurement, 56" finished chest measurement

title: Wheat Vestby Zara Powter
description: A beautiful ode to all things fall, the Wheat Vest reminds us of the soft rustling of the wheat fields and harvest time with its beautifully stacked stitches. Perfect for wearing through multiple seasons, this worsted weight, worked flat, crochet vest will keep you interested while you stitch this unmistakably harvest time pattern. The Wheat Vest is a one-piece seamless vest worked flat in the puffy, Harvest Stitch. The vest is mid-thigh length for extra coziness and an optional button at the top keeps it together. A single crochet edging is crocheted last to finish off the design.
Sizes Included: 36" finished chest measurement, 40" finished chest measurement, 42" finished chest measurement, 46" finished chest measurement, 48" finished chest measurement, 52" finished chest measurement, 54" finished chest measurement, 58" finished chest measurement, 60" finished chest measurement, 64"  finished chest measurement

title: Wildflowers Baby Blanketby Michele DuNaier
description: The Wildflowers Baby Blanket is made with the beautiful colors and exquisite softness of Stroll Tonal yarn. Colorful flower motifs with a cheerful dance of golden shells along the edging make this a one-of-a-kind blanket for a special someone. The smaller square blanket uses 36 motifs and the larger blanket uses 72. After whip-stitching the motifs together, three rounds of edging are worked: a round of SC, then a round of golden shells, and lastly a round of slanted shells for a delicate sawtooth outline.
Sizes Included: Receiving Blanket: 25" x 25", Stroller Blanket: 31 x 35"
Needle / Hook Sizes: US G/6 (4.00mm) Crochet Hook & US F/5 (3.75mm) for last 2 rounds of edging

title: Windrose Rugby Nicoletta Tronci
description: Create your favorite new home decor accent with this elegant rug design made of crochet tapestry squares combining two different patterns. Finish the rug with a simple single crochet border.

title: Windsor Tassel Throwby Two of Wands
description: Drape this regal throw over your favorite chair or at the foot of the bed for a luxe addition to your home. Worked in meditative rounds, this project can easily be sized up or down from a small lapghan to a larger bedspread. To support our Independent Designers , this pattern is excluded from promotions.

title: Winter Cable Hatby Brenda K.B. Anderson
description: ntertwined Cables twist around this cozy hat, designed to keep your noggin warm while you snowshoe through the woods on a crisp winter day. This hat begins with a ribbed band, worked sideways in a long strip, then joined to create a tube. The body of the hat is worked in the round from the ribbing to the top of the hat. The cables are easier than they look, just pay attention to whether your post stitches are worked in front or behind your previous stitches - this will ensure that they cross over each other correctly.

title: Winter Diamonds Market Bagby Jane Howorth
description: The design of this ultra-useful hobo-style market bag is inspired by colorful weavings from Mexico and the southwest US. The handles are just the right length for the bag to feel comfortable on your shoulder or in your hand. Choose your favorite colors to make your own bespoke one-of-a-kind bag. The bag is made from 13 identical motifs which are then crocheted together on the right side to create a decorative ridge seam between motifs. The handles and fastening tab are made amigurumi-style, as tubes, for added strength. A magnetic snap is concealed under the button on the fastening tab for extra security.
Needle / Hook Sizes: US F/5 (3.75mm) and E/4 (3.5mm) Crochet Hooks

title: Winter Waffle Dishclothby Avriel Jackson
description: The Winter Waffle Dishcloth is a square kitchen or bath cloth that is worked flat. It's a quick gift to whip up one or more for a hostess gift or a stocking stuffer. A mixture of Double Crochet and Front Post Double Crochet adds texture and grit with a soft Single Crochet border.

title: Winterberry Cowlby Jessica Herr
description: Indulge in instant warmth and style with this cowl pattern! This project is a quick and satisfying crochet adventure, perfect for beginners and seasoned crafters alike. With just a few stitches, you’ll create an accessory that’s both trendy and practical, making it an essential addition to your winter wardrobe.

title: Wintergreen Chevron Hatby Brenda K.B. Anderson
description: The subtle details in this fun hat are what take it to the next level: stripes of different thicknesses and the zig-zag ribbing really kick it up a notch! Choose strongly contrasting colors for a bolder hat or analogous colors for a more subtle hat.
Sizes Included: 19.5" hat circumference x 9.5" tall, 22.75" hat circumference x 9.5" tall, 26" hat circumference x 11.5" tall

title: Wintry Wreath Ornamentby Kristen Clay
description: These adorable mini wreaths are fast to make and so much fun to customize! Pattern includes a few options, but let your imagination play with ways to decorate these tiny, delicate ornaments.

title: Wisdom Book Bagby Moon Elderidge
description: The Wisdom Book Bag is the perfect bag to hold books and magazines to share with friends when meeting up for a cup of tea. This Tunisian crochet design is worked from the bottom up. The lower section is worked in 2 colors: MC for the forward pass and CC for the return pass. The bag is worked in spiral rounds using double-ended circular Tunisian crochet technique.
Needle / Hook Sizes: G/4.0mm crochet hook , H/5mm Tunisian hook

title: Woodland Trails Hatby Michele DuNaier
description: Here is a fun and fashionable hat to keep you warm as you walk along scenic woodland trails - or on your daily commute to work or school! Beautiful colors and intriguing textures combine to make this a hat you will love to wear. The hat is worked from the crown downward using a variety of interesting stitches - bobbles and zig-zags, reverse single crochet, and double crochets worked between stitches. The earflaps and ties are worked organically as a part of the circular rounds, and the hat is topped with a plump pom pom.
Sizes Included: Small -19" circumference , Medium -20.5" circumference , Large - 22" circumference, XL - 23.5" circumference

title: Woodland Wrap Patternby Kathleen Berlew
description: This simple rectangular wrap wears like a garment. Any space in the fabric’s airy pattern stitch can be used as a buttonhole for a large statement button, allowing the wrap to be worn in a variety of ways. The design's plush block texture is created with a two-row pattern repeat: A single crochet/chain stitch row sets the foundation for the double crochet clusters that are worked into the chain spaces of the subsequent row.

title: Worth Street Afghanby Lion Brand
description: Stunning to look at yet simple to stitch, this beautiful afghan is made of color-changing worsted (4) weight yarn. To support our Independent Designers , this pattern is excluded from promotions.
